# Executive Summary

<!--
This section provides a condensed view of the entire proposal, one page long.
It should be a comprehensive high-level overview that captures the essence of the proposal, including its goals, methods, expected outcomes, deliverables, and budget.
-->

## Overview  
ChatR Skills will develop a modular collection of reusable R-focused skills and MCP-wrapped tools that improve AI assistance for R programming across agentic IDEs such as Claude Code, Cursor, Windsurf, and Codex.

Rather than building a single monolithic assistant, this project will create independent, testable components that help language-model-based agents understand real R workflows. These components will expose structured context about the current R session, datasets, local package libraries, and CRAN package metadata.

## Goals & Methods  
The project will focus on three goals:
- Develop reusable R skills in SKILL.md format.
- Build lightweight MCP-wrapped tools for R session, dataset, package, and CRAN introspection.
- Provide example workflows and tests so mentors can evaluate progress incrementally.

## Outcomes  
By the end of the project, ChatR Skills will provide a practical foundation for context-aware R assistance across multiple agentic environments. The work will be transparent, inspectable, and extensible, allowing future contributors to build additional R skills and tools on top of the same infrastructure.

## Deliverables  
- A reusable set of R skills in SKILL.md format.
- MCP-wrapped tools for session, dataset, package, and CRAN introspection.
- Documentation and usage examples.
- Representative example tasks for ad hoc mentor evaluation.
- A public GitHub repository showing incremental weekly progress.

## Budget  
- **~$3000 total** — ~90% developer effort; ~10% tutorials and community engagement.
